The incredibly massive, deeply humiliating hostage crisis in southern India aggressively continued to paralyze the national security apparatus. In Andhra Pradesh, the state government fiercely battled an absolute, massive ecological catastrophe. ## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On August 4, 1914, the incredibly massive, absolute, and deeply fatal domino effect of World War I was heavily triggered. Following Germany's aggressive, massive invasion of neutral Belgium (the Schlieffen Plan), the United Kingdom officially and fiercely declared war on the German Empire. This massive, monumental decision by London absolutely guaranteed that the European conflict would aggressively explode into a massive, horrific global war, deeply drawing in the entire massive strength of the British Empire and its incredibly vast colonial resources.

On August 4, 1964, a massive, incredibly horrific, and historically pivotal geopolitical incident occurred off the coast of Vietnam. United States Navy destroyers operating in the Gulf of Tonkin aggressively reported a massive, unprovoked attack by North Vietnamese torpedo boats (the highly controversial Gulf of Tonkin incident). This massive, heavily disputed event was aggressively utilized by President Lyndon B. Johnson to heavily secure the massive Gulf of Tonkin Resolution from Congress, absolutely launching the incredibly massive, devastating American military escalation in the Vietnam War.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On August 4, 1956, the incredibly massive, highly historic, and technologically foundational Apsara nuclear research reactor was officially and aggressively activated at Trombay. Heavily designed by the brilliant Homi J. Bhabha, it became the absolute, very first nuclear reactor in India and the entire continent of Asia to achieve massive criticality. This incredibly massive engineering triumph heavily laid the absolute, architectural bedrock for the massive, highly advanced, and deeply independent Indian civil and military nuclear program that continues to fiercely secure the nation today.

On August 4, 1935, Gangadharrao Deshpande, the incredibly massive, highly revered Indian independence activist, and fiercely devoted follower of Mahatma Gandhi, officially passed away. Deeply respected as the "Lion of Karnataka," his massive, uncompromising efforts in heavily organizing the massive Non-Cooperation Movement and his absolute dedication to massive Khadi initiatives heavily galvanized the Indian masses in the southern regions, deeply cementing his legacy as an absolute, foundational pillar of the incredibly massive, subcontinent-wide struggle for absolute freedom from the British Raj.
